The **Kingdom of Portugal**[[3]](#cite_note-7) was a [monarchy](/wiki/Portuguese_monarchy "Portuguese monarchy") in the western [Iberian Peninsula](/wiki/Iberian_Peninsula "Iberian Peninsula") and the predecessor of the modern [Portuguese Republic](/wiki/Portuguese_Republic "Portuguese Republic"). Existing to various extents between the mid-12th century and the early 20th century, it was also known as the **Kingdom of Portugal and the Algarves** after 1471, and was the main constituent of the [United Kingdom of Portugal, Brazil and the Algarves](/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Portugal,_Brazil_and_the_Algarves "United Kingdom of Portugal, Brazil and the Algarves"), which existed between 1815 and 1822. It coexisted with the [Portuguese Empire](/wiki/Portuguese_Empire "Portuguese Empire"), the realm's overseas colonies.

The nucleus of the Portuguese state was the [County of Portugal](/wiki/County_of_Portugal "County of Portugal"), established in the 9th century as part of the *[Reconquista](/wiki/Reconquista "Reconquista")* by [Vímara Peres](/wiki/V%C3%ADmara_Peres "Vímara Peres"), a vassal of the [King of Asturias](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Asturias "Kingdom of Asturias"). The county became part of the [Kingdom of León](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Le%C3%B3n "Kingdom of León") in 1097, and the Counts of Portugal established themselves as rulers of an independent kingdom in the 12th century, following the [Battle of São Mamede](/wiki/Battle_of_S%C3%A3o_Mamede "Battle of São Mamede"). The kingdom was ruled by the [Afonsine Dynasty](/wiki/Portuguese_House_of_Burgundy "Portuguese House of Burgundy") until the [1383–85 Crisis](/wiki/1383%E2%80%9385_Crisis "1383–85 Crisis"), after which the monarchy passed to the [House of Aviz](/wiki/House_of_Aviz "House of Aviz").

During the 15th and 16th century, [Portuguese exploration](/wiki/Portuguese_discoveries "Portuguese discoveries") established a vast [colonial empire](/wiki/Portuguese_Empire "Portuguese Empire"). From 1580 to 1640, the Kingdom of Portugal was in [personal union](/wiki/Iberian_Union "Iberian Union") with [Habsburg Spain](/wiki/Habsburg_Spain "Habsburg Spain").

After the [Portuguese Restoration War](/wiki/Portuguese_Restoration_War "Portuguese Restoration War") of 1640–1668, the kingdom passed to the [House of Braganza](/wiki/House_of_Braganza "House of Braganza") and thereafter to the [House of Braganza-Saxe-Coburg and Gotha](/wiki/House_of_Braganza-Saxe-Coburg_and_Gotha "House of Braganza-Saxe-Coburg and Gotha"). From this time, the influence of Portugal declined, but it remained a major power due to its most valuable colony, [Brazil](/wiki/Colonial_Brazil "Colonial Brazil"). After the [independence of Brazil](/wiki/Independence_of_Brazil "Independence of Brazil"), Portugal sought to establish itself [in Africa](/wiki/Scramble_for_Africa "Scramble for Africa"), but was ultimately forced to halt its expansion due to the [1890 British Ultimatum](/wiki/British_Ultimatum_of_1890 "British Ultimatum of 1890"), eventually leading to the collapse of the monarchy in the [5 October 1910 revolution](/wiki/5_October_1910_revolution "5 October 1910 revolution") and the establishment of the [First Portuguese Republic](/wiki/First_Portuguese_Republic "First Portuguese Republic").

Portugal was an [absolute monarchy](/wiki/Absolute_monarchy "Absolute monarchy") before 1822. It alternated between absolute and [semi-constitutional monarchy](/wiki/Semi-constitutional_monarchy "Semi-constitutional monarchy") from 1822 until 1834, when it would remain a semi-constitutional monarchy until its fall.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Portugal&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### Origins

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Portugal&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Origins")]

The Kingdom of Portugal finds its origins in the [County of Portugal](/wiki/County_of_Portugal "County of Portugal"). The Portuguese County was a semi-autonomous county of the [Kingdom of León](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Le%C3%B3n "Kingdom of León"). Independence from León took place in three stages:

1. The first on 26 July 1139 when [Afonso Henriques](/wiki/Afonso_I_of_Portugal "Afonso I of Portugal") was acclaimed [King of the Portuguese](/wiki/List_of_Portuguese_monarchs "List of Portuguese monarchs") by his troops after the (possibly legendary)[[4]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTEFerreira201024-8)[[5]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTESmith199663-9) [Battle of Ourique](/wiki/Battle_of_Ourique "Battle of Ourique").[[6]](#cite_note-10)
2. The second was on 5 October 1143, when [Alfonso VII of León and Castile](/wiki/Alfonso_VII_of_Le%C3%B3n_and_Castile "Alfonso VII of León and Castile") recognized Afonso Henriques as king through the [Treaty of Zamora](/wiki/Treaty_of_Zamora "Treaty of Zamora"). This is generally considered the traditional founding of the kingdom in Portuguese historiography and popular imagination.
3. The third, in 1179, was the [Papal Bull](/wiki/Papal_bull "Papal bull") *[Manifestis Probatum](/wiki/Manifestis_Probatum "Manifestis Probatum")*, in which Portugal's independence was recognized by [Pope Alexander III](/wiki/Pope_Alexander_III "Pope Alexander III").

Once Portugal was independent, D. Afonso I's descendants, members of the [Portuguese House of Burgundy](/wiki/Portuguese_House_of_Burgundy "Portuguese House of Burgundy"), would rule Portugal until 1383. Even after the change in royal houses, all the monarchs of Portugal were descended from Afonso I, one way or another, through both legitimate and illegitimate links.

### Fall of the Monarchy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Portugal&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Fall of the Monarchy")]

Further information: [House of Braganza-Saxe-Coburg and Gotha](/wiki/House_of_Braganza-Saxe-Coburg_and_Gotha "House of Braganza-Saxe-Coburg and Gotha"), [Lisbon Regicide](/wiki/Lisbon_Regicide "Lisbon Regicide"), and [5 October 1910 revolution](/wiki/5_October_1910_revolution "5 October 1910 revolution")

With the start of the 20th century, Republicanism grew in numbers and support in Lisbon among progressive politicians and the influential press. However a minority with regard to the rest of the country, this height of republicanism would benefit politically from the [Lisbon Regicide](/wiki/Lisbon_Regicide "Lisbon Regicide") on 1 February 1908. While returning from the [Ducal Palace](/wiki/Ducal_Palace_of_Vila_Vi%C3%A7osa "Ducal Palace of Vila Viçosa") at [Vila Viçosa](/wiki/Vila_Vi%C3%A7osa "Vila Viçosa"), [King Charles](/wiki/Carlos_I_of_Portugal "Carlos I of Portugal") and the [Prince Royal](/wiki/Prince_Royal_of_Portugal "Prince Royal of Portugal") [Luís Filipe](/wiki/Lu%C3%ADs_Filipe,_Prince_Royal_of_Portugal "Luís Filipe, Prince Royal of Portugal") were assassinated in the [Terreiro do Paço](/wiki/Terreiro_do_Pa%C3%A7o "Terreiro do Paço"), in [Lisbon](/wiki/Lisbon "Lisbon"). With the death of the King and his heir, Charles I's second son became monarch as [King Manuel II](/wiki/Manuel_II_of_Portugal "Manuel II of Portugal"). Manuel's reign was short-lived however, ending by force with the [5 October 1910 revolution](/wiki/5_October_1910_revolution "5 October 1910 revolution"), forcing the deposed king into immediate exile in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Great_Britain_and_Ireland "United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland") and giving way to the [Portuguese First Republic](/wiki/Portuguese_First_Republic "Portuguese First Republic").

On 19 January 1919, the [Monarchy of the North](/wiki/Monarchy_of_the_North "Monarchy of the North") was proclaimed in [Porto](/wiki/Porto "Porto"), but its inability to gain strong popular support anywhere in the rest the country, coupled with its unorganised structure, led to its swift demise and the re-establishment of republican control in the north a month later.[[7]](#cite_note-LisboaRev-11) No other monarchist counter-revolution in Portugal has occurred since.

After the republican revolution in October 1910, the remaining colonies of the empire became overseas provinces of the [Portuguese Republic](/wiki/Portuguese_Republic "Portuguese Republic") until the late 20th century when the last overseas territories of Portugal became independent, most notably in [Portuguese Africa](/wiki/Portuguese_Empire "Portuguese Empire") where the provinces of [Angola](/wiki/Portuguese_Angola "Portuguese Angola") and [Mozambique](/wiki/Portuguese_Mozambique "Portuguese Mozambique") achieved independence in 1975. The [Portuguese Empire](/wiki/Portuguese_Empire "Portuguese Empire") ended definitively with the handover of [Macau](/wiki/Portuguese_Macau "Portuguese Macau") to [China](/wiki/China "China") in 1999.

## Footnotes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kingdom_of_Portugal&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Footnotes")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-1)** also known as the [Kingdom of Portugal and the Algarves](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Portugal_and_the_Algarves "Kingdom of Portugal and the Algarves") (Latin: *Regnum Portugalliae et Algarbiae*, Portuguese: *Reino de Portugal e dos Algarves*) after 1415, and as the [United Kingdom of Portugal, Brazil and the Algarves](/wiki/United_Kingdom_of_Portugal,_Brazil_and_the_Algarves "United Kingdom of Portugal, Brazil and the Algarves") (Portuguese: *Reino Unido de Portugal, Brasil e Algarves*) between 1815 and 1822.
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** From 1808 to 1821 [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro") [was the seat of the Portuguese court and the *de facto* seat of government](/wiki/Transfer_of_the_Portuguese_court_to_Brazil "Transfer of the Portuguese court to Brazil"). Additionally, [Antony I](/wiki/Antony_I_of_Portugal "Antony I of Portugal") and [Mary II](/wiki/Mary_II_of_Portugal "Mary II of Portugal") ruled from [Angra do Heroísmo](/wiki/Angra_do_Hero%C3%ADsmo "Angra do Heroísmo") between 1580 to 1582 and 1830 to 1834 respectively whilst their holds over [the mainland](/wiki/Mainland_Portugal "Mainland Portugal") were in dispute.
3. **[^](#cite_ref-3)** [Galician-Portuguese](/wiki/Galician-Portuguese "Galician-Portuguese") (until 16th century)  
   [Modern Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language") (16th century onward)
4. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** Widely used for administrative and liturgical purposes. [Medieval Latin](/wiki/Medieval_Latin "Medieval Latin") replaced by [Renaissance Latin](/wiki/Renaissance_Latin "Renaissance Latin") by the 15th century.
